title: digitalis ferruginea subsp. ferruginea
intro: Digitalis ferruginea subsp. ferruginea, also known as the rusty foxglove, is a subspecies of Digitalis ferruginea, a type of flowering plant native to the mountains of southern Europe and North Africa. The plant contains cardiac glycosides, which have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, particularly in treating heart conditions such as atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure. The health benefits of Digitalis ferruginea subsp. ferruginea include its ability to improve cardiac function, reduce blood pressure, and prevent cardiac arrhythmias. Digitalis ferruginea subsp. ferruginea can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or decoction for medicinal use, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy.
